blocks.content: Cassandra pressed on — not angrily, but precisely, with the careful weight of someone who had been thinking about this longer than this conversation.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: I understand why you're scared. I understand the version of this that you see from the outside. But from the outside, the safest choice always looks like the right one, and it's not the same from in here.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: I'm not asking you to stop living.
Maya's voice shifted — lower, something more vulnerable surfacing beneath the measured surface.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: I'm asking you to be careful.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: I'm asking you not to disappear into something and then—

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: You're not just protecting me. You're protecting yourself. In case.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: I know you are, and I'm not saying that as a criticism. I understand it completely. But I can't make my choices based on how to make the loss easier. Because that's what being careful feels like from my side. Like practicing the end before anything has even started.
Maya turned her cup slowly on the table. Her jaw held tight in the way it did when she was not going to let herself cry.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: I just don't want you to get hurt.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: But being alive includes getting hurt. That's the deal. And I made a choice a long time ago that I was going to be alive for all of it — not just the safe parts.
Not an accusation. Just a fact she was naming.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: When are you going to tell him?
Maya looked at her for a long moment. Then nodded — the slow, accepting nod that meant she wasn't going to fight anymore, even if she wanted to.
